# Example Profile: Deno Weekly

- profile_name: deno-weekly
- repository: https://github.com/denoland/deno.git
- branch: main
- period_days: 7
- output_path: deno-weekly-newsletter.md
- title_style: Deno Runtime Weekly

Editorial focus:
- Prioritize runtime behavior, Node compatibility, deployment tooling, package management, permissions model changes, and developer productivity wins.
- De-emphasize low-signal cleanup unless it changes the day-to-day experience of building and shipping Deno apps.
- Deep-dive candidates: compatibility strategy, deploy workflows, runtime APIs, and security model updates.

Section preferences:
- Keep sections in this order: Runtime Surface, Compatibility, Deploy and Tooling, Performance, Developer Notes.
- Include a short "Worth trying this week" subsection for especially usable improvements.

Tone and format:
- Audience: full-stack JavaScript and TypeScript teams evaluating or actively using Deno.
- Keep the tone breezy but grounded, with concrete language about readiness and workflow changes.
